description: Per sample processing for 1 audio channels

properties.note: UI-only: controls whether the container is displayed vertically or horizontally in the scriptnode graph. No effect on audio processing.

gaps.question: frame1_block uses wrap::frame<1, T> which is fix<1, frame_x<T>>. How does the interleaving work with only 1 channel? Is the child's processFrame() called with a span<float, 1>, and does the child see blockSize=1 in its PrepareSpecs?
gaps.context: Users need to understand that children inside frame1_block receive one sample at a time on one channel. This determines which child nodes are compatible.

gaps.question: If the parent network has 2 channels but frame1_block fixes to 1 channel, what happens to the second channel? The infrastructure doc mentions SingleSampleBlock handles channel mismatch with a leftover buffer. How does this leftover mechanism work?
gaps.context: Critical for stereo networks. Users placing frame1_block in a stereo context need to know whether the second channel is discarded, passed through unprocessed, or buffered.

gaps.question: When frame1_block is bypassed, does it revert to block processing (passing through to children with original block size)? The containers infrastructure doc mentions SingleSampleBlock overrides setBypassed() to re-prepare.
gaps.context: Bypass behaviour for frame containers differs from block containers. Need to confirm the mechanism.

gaps.question: The existing doc warns of 'significant CPU overhead'. What is the nature of this overhead -- is it purely from per-sample function call overhead, or does the interleave/deinterleave add measurable cost? How does it compare to fix8_block?
gaps.context: The existing doc recommends fix8_block as a compromise. Quantifying the overhead ratio helps users make informed decisions.

notes: Base variant of the frameN_block group. Uses wrap::frame<1, T> = wrap::fix<1, wrap::frame_x<T>>. This is the mono per-sample processing container. The existing phase3 doc is excellent -- 24 lines explaining the buffer abstraction, when per-sample processing is needed, CPU overhead warning, and the fix8_block compromise recommendation. Key distinction from framex_block: frame1_block fixes channel count to 1 at compile time, while framex_block uses dynamic channel count.
